大嶼山東涌赤立角村發生駭人聽聞的懷疑人獸交案，事件中一隻唐狗女下體撕裂流血，警方指人獸交屬違法，案情嚴重，今日拘捕一名50餘歲男子。事發於本月初，有村民發現疑人以食物引誘白色唐狗女上山，數小時後唐狗女下體流血現身，由村民救走送獸醫診所檢驗，發現陰道遺下懷疑人類精液，相信狗女遭性侵報警。


事件中遭侵犯的唐狗女3歲大，據知事後唐狗女對陌生人十分慌張，警方已帶走唐狗女檢驗。至於被捕男子姓鍾(58歲)，報稱任職地盤工人，住東涌，警方列人獸交案。消息稱，事發的東涌赤立角村一帶有一批流浪狗出沒，常有村民餵飼，本月1日下午，有村民到附近黃龍坑道餵飼流浪狗，其間發現一名50餘歲可疑男子出現，暗中留意。


該可疑男子其後取出食物引誘一頭白色唐狗女，未幾白色唐狗女隨他上山，該村民曾在場找尋，但可疑人與唐狗女同告不知所終，他擔心唐狗女安危留下等待。數小時後唐狗女再現身，但當時下體流血，不停哀鳴，可疑人則下落不明。村民救走狗女帶到私家獸醫診所求醫，獸醫初步檢查發現唐狗女陰道撕裂，並遺留懷疑精液物體，獸醫取出懷疑精液樣本化驗初步相信屬人類精液，估計狗女曾遭侵犯報警求助。


警方指事態嚴重，即時派警員到赤立角村一帶調查，搜捕可疑男子，同時帶走涉事唐狗女檢驗，另外警方從獸醫診所取走相關懷疑精液樣本交政府化驗所詳細化驗，確認是否人類精液。


警方連日調查後，初步鎖定一名居於東涌逸東邨男子懷疑涉案，至今日到疑人寓所將他拘捕帶署。

Emi Martinez says it’s his goal to become the number 1 goalkeeper at Arsenal, despite the fact he’s currently third in the pecking order behind Petr Cech and David Ospina.

The 24 year old isn’t fazed though, and says he won’t rest until he’s fulfilled his ambition.

Sounding like a villain in a superhero movie, he told Arsenal Player, “Year by year I’ve been growing, getting bigger, more powerful and so I thought, ‘Why not?’

“You expect to be in the squad at the end of the week and I’m not, so that’s why I have to do something about it. The chance will come so I need to be ready.

“I always believe in myself,” he continued. “I was a big player at my former club in Argentina so I can be a big player here. I believe in that and I won’t stop until I’m the Arsenal No 1.”

And on his time at the club that he joined as a teenager, he said it’s a big part of why he wants to make it.

“Arsenal is like a family,” he said. “It’s my club and I’ve been here for seven years so it feels like home. I’m here early for breakfast and I’m one of the last to leave.

“I really enjoy it here so that’s the reason why I have to be positive.”

You really can’t fault the attitude, but he’s got some amount of work to do to make that position his.

Martinez has made three EFL Cup appearances this season, but has yet to feature in a Premier League game in this current campaign.

Wenger on his future and Wilshere

With his contract up at the end of the season, Arsène Wenger has long been fielding questions about what the future may hold for him.

Once again, the Arsenal manager was asked about it in Thursday’s pre-match press conference and this is what he had to say:

on whether a contract is on the table for him…
Once again, thank you for caring about my future, but that’s not the most important thing. My future is in front of me every time I make a half-turn. What is important for me is that Arsenal Football Club does well, and I dedicate my whole energy to the club doing well.

on what the latest is…
I answer that question every week and nothing has changed compared to last week. That’s basically it.

on whether he has made a decision…
I have nothing to add to what I said last week.

on what factors will influence his decision on his future…
Ideally you want everybody to be happy. But as well, I think Arsenal is made of special strength and that strength as well is to be united when it doesn’t go well and that’s what we want. It’s very nice for you to care about how I feel, but it’s not the most important. I am a professional and I have to do a job and that’s what I want to do.

on discussions about Jack Wilshere’s future with Bournemouth…
No, no.

on whether he expects Jack to return at the end of his loan spell…
Yes. We have, at the moment, had no move from Bournemouth for Jack Wilshere.

on Laurent Koscielny’s comments post-Chelsea…
The comments that have been made were lost in translation. They have been twisted. But anyway, the players are responsible for their performance. The club works well when everybody does his job. The players are responsible for their performance and that’s all, not more than that. I believe the manager is responsible for the results and the team selection, the board is responsible for long-term decisions and the club works only like that.

on whether he asked Laurent about the quotes attributed to him…
Yes, of course. He didn’t mean that at all. When you have a disappointing result, you know what you get. People turn things around and we are used to that. We can deal with that.

on his analysis of what’s happened in last two weeks…
It happened because we played at Chelsea and it’s a difficult game. It’s down to the fact that we did not perform as well as we wanted, especially defensively. We made some mistakes that, as a team, you cannot afford to in these kinds of games.

on whether he has been surprised by the defeats…
I’m always surprised when we lose a game because I expect to win it before the game starts. We were also a bit unlucky because the first goal was absolutely scandalous. That changed the game as well because we started quite well, but from that point onwards it put Chelsea in a position that they are very good at. They defend very well and they are very quick on the break. When you look at how the second goal happened, I think we can only look at ourselves, and for the third goal as well. Overall it was a very even game on goal chances. The regret I have is that we didn’t make enough of the chances we created.

Allegri to leave Juventus?
By Football Italia staff

It’s reported that Max Allegri and Juventus agreed in January to part ways at the end of the season.

Rumours have been growing in recent weeks that the Coach will leave after this season, and last night he refused to confirm or deny that he could move to Arsenal.

Now calciomercato.com is reporting that a mutual decision was taken in January for Allegri to leave the Bianconeri this summer.

The website notes that there has been no falling out or bitterness, but both the club and the Coach feel that the current cycle is coming to an end.

Since Marcello Lippi’s first spell on the bench, no Juve Coach has gone beyond three seasons in Turin.

Fiorentina’s Paulo Sousa is a candidate to replace him, while there are even sensational rumours Luciano Spalletti could leave Roma in order to take over.

Spalletti's contract is up at the end of the current campaign, and so far he has not signed a new deal.

　　「我的執教生涯中已經經歷了超過2000場比賽，在每一場比賽過後，我都會自己來做一次深入的分析。同時，我還會在電視上看許多比賽，也會對這些比賽進行評論和分析。我有足夠的能力，我知道怎麼拋除個人情感因素來客觀地分析一件事情，我也能充分理解在比賽中什麼是有效果的，什麼是沒有效果的。」

大部份香港人就是一個遵循傳統、習慣地執行，卻同時質疑、責難的一個奇怪人種。不論外圍環境如何轉變，都會用同一個口吻、同一種方法來應對。口裡經常說：「XX下台XX便會好一點」云云的說話。多人講，聲音當然大聲，然後自恃人多而分隔與自己意見不同的人，漠視他們的聲音。因而忽略其他重要的因素，然後執迷在自己所行的路上。最後幾乎沒有人能夠聽聽其他聲音、想想「改變」之後，環境是怎樣、制度是怎樣、未來可以怎樣？

不要緊，反正遲早老退的雲加，他退休後的兩三季，各大阿仙奴球迷自然會清醒地更加沮喪，就如後費格遜時代的曼聯那樣。
